step.title: Pre-Flight Subagent Dispatch
step.goal: Dispatch all subagents flagged in the Writing Plan in PARALLEL before writing begins. Collect findings into session memory keyed by section name. This batches the I/O so step 3 can write sections without per-section blocking.

substep.title: Collect Dispatch List
substep.action:
Walk the Writing Plan's dispatch_plan. Collect every section that has needs_dispatch: true into a single dispatch list. Each entry: {section_name, subagent, prompt_hint}.
If no section needs dispatch, this step is a no-op; skip to substep 2e.

substep.title: Compose Subagent Prompts
substep.action:
For each dispatch entry, expand the prompt_hint from the plan into a full subagent prompt. Each prompt: 1. Names the question to answer (derived from the section's scope note) 2. Lists the constraint (e.g., "return file paths only; do not analyze") 3. Specifies output shape (e.g., "markdown table with file:line and one-line purpose")
Store the expanded prompts in {{dispatch_prompts}}.

substep.title: Dispatch in Parallel
substep.action:
Dispatch ALL prompts in a single message via the Task tool. Multiple Task calls in ONE message = parallel execution. Wait for ALL to return before proceeding.
For each return, synthesize the finding into a brief markdown block tagged with the section_name. Store as {{section_findings[section_name]}}.

substep.title: Display Dispatch Summary

section.title: Parallel dispatch is mandatory
section.description: Like ticket-grill's step 4, dispatch happens in a SINGLE message with multiple Task calls. This is what makes them parallel. Serial dispatch defeats the efficiency win of pre-flighting.

section.title: When no dispatches are planned
section.description: Some tickets need zero write-time research — all needed evidence was gathered during ticket-grill. That's normal. Step 2 becomes a no-op; section writing proceeds directly to step 3.

section.title: Findings synthesis discipline
section.description: Subagent returns are SYNTHESIZED into per-section findings. Each finding is one short markdown block that names the evidence (file paths, URLs, prior thoughts) without pasting raw subagent output. The synthesis is what step 3 writes into the ticket body.
